evidence: CKAF Autumn closes 1 September 2026 at 11:00.
The route is for UK registered government research organisations to move public sector Knowledge Assets towards commercial readiness.
The lead must be a PRSE, UKRI wholly owned institute or other public sector body that undertakes research as a primary function, with KA ownership and senior support.
GOV.UK guidance says CKAF grants are for government research organisations and lists grantfund.gott@dsit.gov.uk for questions.
DRIVE35 Collaborate 3 closes 16 September 2026 at 11:00.

The route requires late-stage collaborative R&D, a GBP2.5 million to GBP20 million grant request, at least 50% match funding, a UK business lead, collaboration, and a vehicle manufacturer or Tier 1 supplier in the consortium.
APC lists a general enquiries contact route at info@apcuk.co.uk.
ZEVI 2 closes 16 September 2026 at 11:00 and funds clean maritime solutions for three years in a real world environment.
Energy efficiency strand requirements include total eligible costs between GBP6 million and GBP60 million, maximum grant request GBP30 million, and a non-funded three-year demonstration.
IFS notes the National Shipbuilding Office can be used in an advisory capacity for UK shipyard or boat builder capability.
Quantum hardware CR&D closes 18 September 2026 at 11:00.
Projects need a GBP1 million to GBP3.5 million grant request, a 24 to 36 month term, and a UK business or RTO lead for collaborative projects.
IFS encourages co-development with industrial partners and full stack integrators of quantum computers in the UK.
UK National Quantum Technologies Programme lists QCi3 as a quantum computing hub and gives engage.qci3@physics.ox.ac.uk as contact.
DRIVE35 Scale Up has no submission deadline and supports zero emission vehicle manufacturing facility and process development.
Projects must request GBP2.5 million to GBP20 million grant funding, have total eligible costs of at least GBP5 million, start from 1 March 2027, and raise co-investment after a grant offer.
A UK registered business may lead; RTOs and academic institutions cannot lead or work alone.
Innovation Loans EOI is a loan route with no submission deadline.
Loans can be GBP100,000 to GBP5 million and applicants must show they can cover interest and repay on time.
The route is limited to UK registered SMEs; only single businesses can receive loans.

route_actions.reason_no_direct_submission: Current evidence does not include an eligible PRSE, UKRI wholly owned institute or qualifying public sector research organisation lead, owned Knowledge Asset, senior support letter, IP rights evidence or no-subsidy role approval.

Current evidence does not include an eligible UK automotive lead, vehicle manufacturer or Tier 1 supplier, GBP2.5m plus grant request, 50% match funding, TRL/MRL evidence, partner budgets or consortium invitations.

Current evidence does not include an eligible maritime lead, vessel/port/harbour/infrastructure owner, GBP6m plus eligible costs, grant/match budget, permits, shipyard route or three-year demonstration commitment.
Current evidence does not include a quantum hardware business or RTO lead, consortium, GBP1m plus grant budget, hardware/facility access, partner budgets or performance roadmap evidence.

Current evidence does not include a zero emission vehicle manufacturing facility, GBP5m plus eligible costs, GBP2.5m plus grant request, co-investment plan, capital investment evidence or delivery capacity.

This is repayable finance, not a grant. Current evidence does not include action-time approval, repayment capacity evidence, interest coverage, loan amount, credit risk pack or Windsor Framework position.

controls: No IFS portal application was opened, mutated, submitted, uploaded, invited or accepted.
No unsupported eligible lead, partner, facility, vessel, port, budget, match funding, co-investment, repayment capacity, permit, IP ownership or hardware access claim was made.
Formal email bodies do not contain internal draft-control language; draft-only state is stored in headers and ledgers.
Innovation Loans remains outside the grant-submission queue until repayment evidence and exact action-time approval exist.
